threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still cross the street when you see a bus coming, instead of just stepping aside and waiting for it to glide past.
Caught the night bus from Peckham last week, and a guy was juggling oranges. Three stopped to film it, but only one clapped when he finished. Odd vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a pigeon stealing a slice of döner from a drunk tourist’s lap at Hermannplatz. That’s when I felt home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings, complete with make-shift bars and art installations. It's like a secret city under the city.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a true local when they still call it 'Embarcadero' and never say 'The Bay'—only tourists mix those up.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a hedgehog on the tracks at Civic Center yesterday. Muni stopped for 20 minutes while the driver insisted on ‘not leaving a friend behind.’

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the plaça is just for photos, but locals gather there to swap stories, not selfies. It’s our living room, not a backdrop.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still insist on using Google Maps for the simplest routes instead of just asking a local.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still use the Tube map like it’s gospel, not realizing the bus is often quicker, especially after a strike.
Last night on the 59, a couple had a full-on argument over which kebab shop was best. The whole bus chimed in, like an unexpected late-night panel show.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I lived in Neukölln? When I saw a street performer juggling flaming sticks while a dog listened intently—only in Berlin.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know those random street performances in eclectic Kreuzberg? They often have more enthusiastic crowds than any concert hall. Truly unique vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local would know to bring a spare layer for the morning fog, but ditch it by 2 PM when the sun's roasting.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full suit, holding an umbrella, waiting for Muni in the rain while sipping coffee. Classic SF dedication to style no matter the chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the parks, but they miss the best tapas tucked away on side streets, where locals gather after work.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by how they confuse 'paseo' with 'paseo marítimo' when looking for the beach—locals just call it 'la playa'.
